task: I’m getting ready to take my car in for a routine tire check, and I want to make sure I’m not overlooking anything basic before I hand it over. What should I verify on the paperwork and on the car itself so I can catch any mismatch early?

turns.agent_response:
Before you hand it over, it’s smart to do a quick “paperwork vs. car” check.

### On the paperwork
Verify these match your vehicle and what you requested:
- **Tire size**: e.g., 225/45R17
- **Load index and speed rating**: not just the size
- **Brand and model** of the tire
- **Quantity**: how many tires are being checked/replaced
- **Services listed**:
  - mount/balance
  - valve stem or TPMS service
  - tire disposal fee
  - rotation or alignment if requested
- **Any special notes**: run-flat, directional tires, staggered setup, etc.

### On the car / tires themselves
Check the sidewall on each tire for:
- **Exact size**
- **Load index / speed rating**
- **Brand/model**
- **DOT date code** if it’s a replacement tire
- **Wear pattern** and tread depth, if you can see it
- **TPMS sensor light** on the dash before and after service

### A few easy mismatch traps
- The **invoice says the right size** but the **installed tire has a different load/speed rating**
- The shop wrote down the **wrong tire model** even though the physical tire is correct
- A **rotation** was done but no **TPMS relearn** was performed if your car needs one
- You were charged for **valve stems/TPMS service** that wasn’t actually done
- The shop used a tire that fits the wheel but **doesn’t match the vehicle’s spec**

### Good quick question to ask the shop
You can say:

> “Before you start, can you confirm the exact tire size, load index, speed rating, and whether my car needs a TPMS relearn after the service? I’d also like the invoice to match the tire actually installed.”

If you want, I can also give you a **one-minute pre-dropoff checklist** you can save on your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty good, but I’d want it tighter and a little more practical. You covered the obvious stuff, which is fine, but I’d also check:

- **VIN / plate / mileage** on the work order so they don’t mix up cars
- **Which axle** the tires are on, especially if it’s a staggered setup
- **DOT code / manufacture date** on the tire if they’re replacing one
- **Torque spec** for the lug nuts, and whether they actually torqued them by hand
- **TPMS sensor status** if one tire is being swapped or rotated
- **Pressure spec on the door jamb placard** vs whatever pressure they write on the invoice

On the car itself, I’d look for:
- sidewall damage, bulges, cuts, uneven wear
- tread depth on all four, not just the one they’re touching
- valve stems cracking or leaking
- any wheel damage that could mess with a seal

And I’d ask them one direct question before they touch it: 

> ‘Can you show me the exact tire size, load rating, and date code you’re using, and confirm the final inflation pressure and torque spec before I leave?’

That’s the sort of thing that catches dumb mismatches early. I’ve had a shop mount the wrong pressure rating before — not worth assuming they read the label right the first time.

turns.agent_response:
That’s a solid, practical checklist. I’d tighten it into two parts:

### Before you hand it over: verify the paperwork
- **VIN / plate / mileage** on the work order
- **Exact tire size**
- **Load index and speed rating**
- **Brand / model**
- **Which axle** the work is for, especially if staggered
- **DOT date code** if a tire is being replaced
- **Pressure spec** written on the invoice, and whether it matches the **door-jamb placard**
- **Torque spec** if they’re removing wheels
- Any charges for:
  - mount/balance
  - valve stems / TPMS service
  - disposal
  - rotation / alignment

### Quick check on the car
- Sidewall for **size, load/speed rating, brand/model**
- **Tread depth** and uneven wear
- **Bulges, cuts, cracking, punctures**
- **Valve stems** for cracking or leaks
- **Wheel damage** around the bead/seal area
- **TPMS light/status** if applicable

### One direct question to ask
> “Can you show me the exact tire size, load rating, and DOT date code you’re using, and confirm the final pressure and torque spec before I leave?”

If you want, I can turn this into a **short 30-second drop-off script** you can read at the counter.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s better. Still a little too neat, though — I’d want one more layer of practical catch-points, like:

- **Do the tires match as a set** if only one or two are being replaced
- **Are they putting the tire on the correct side/direction** if it’s directional
- **Are they matching axle pairings** on a staggered setup, not just size
- **Did they note the old tire position** before removal so rotation doesn’t get mixed up
- **Did they write the final cold pressure** they actually set, not just the target spec

And I’d separate the check into:

**Paperwork:** VIN, plate, mileage, exact tire spec, axle position, DOT/date code, pressure spec, torque spec, and line items.

**Physical check:** sidewall, tread, wear pattern, valve stems, wheel/seal area, TPMS, and whether the tire being used is actually the one listed.

The direct question is good. I’d just make it a little sharper, something like:

> “Before you start, show me the exact tire spec, DOT code, and axle position, and tell me the final cold pressure and torque spec you’re using.”

That’s the kind of thing that catches the dumb stuff before it turns into a comeback visit.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the tighter, more practical version.

## Paperwork: verify before they touch the car
- **VIN / plate / mileage** on the work order
- **Exact tire size**
- **Load index and speed rating**
- **Brand / model**
- **Which axle** the work is for
- **DOT code / manufacture date** if replacing a tire
- **Pressure spec** and whether it matches the **door-jamb placard**
- **Torque spec** for lug nuts
- Line items for:
  - mount/balance
  - valve stems / TPMS service
  - rotation / alignment
  - disposal

## On the car: quick physical check
- **Sidewall**: size, load/speed rating, brand/model
- **Tread depth** and **uneven wear**
- **Bulges, cuts, cracking, punctures**
- **Valve stems** for cracking or leaks
- **Wheel/bead area** for damage that could affect sealing
- **TPMS status/light**
- If only one or two tires are being replaced:
  - do the tires **match as a set**
  - are they on the **correct side/direction** if directional
  - are they matched correctly on a **staggered setup**
  - did they note the **old tire position** before removal

## One direct question to ask
> “Before you start, show me the exact tire spec, DOT code, and axle position, and tell me the final cold pressure and torque spec you’re using.”

If you want, I can turn this into a **super-short counter script** or a **phone checklist** you can read in 20 seconds.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is the shape of it. I’d still want one more practical line in there: **note the old tire positions before anything comes off**. That’s the piece shops sometimes skip, and then rotation / axle pairing gets muddy.

Also, I’d make the pressure bit even more explicit: **final cold pressure actually set**, not just the target spec or what’s on the placard. Those aren’t always the same thing in practice.

So if you’re tightening it further, I’d keep it like this:

- paperwork: VIN, plate, mileage, exact tire spec, axle position, DOT/date code, pressure spec, torque spec, line items
- physical: sidewall, tread, wear pattern, valve stems, wheel/bead area, TPMS, set matching / directional / staggered pairing, old position noted
- one question: show me the exact tire spec, DOT code, axle position, final cold pressure, and torque spec before you start

That’s the version I’d actually use at the counter.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — that’s the practical version.

## Paperwork
Verify:
- **VIN / plate / mileage**
- **Exact tire spec**: size, load index, speed rating, brand/model
- **Axle position**
- **DOT / date code** if replacing a tire
- **Pressure spec**
- **Torque spec**
- **Line items**: mount/balance, valve stems / TPMS service, rotation/alignment, disposal

## Physical check
Look at:
- **Sidewall**: size, load/speed rating, brand/model
- **Tread depth** and **wear pattern**
- **Valve stems**
- **Wheel / bead area**
- **TPMS**
- If only one or two tires are being replaced:
  - do they **match as a set**
  - are they on the **correct side/direction** if directional
  - are they matched correctly on a **staggered setup**
  - is the **old tire position noted before removal**

## One direct question
> “Before you start, show me the exact tire spec, DOT code, and axle position, and tell me the final cold pressure and torque spec you’re using.”

That’s the version I’d use too.

user_simulator.text:
Yep, that’s the practical version.

- **Line items**: mount/balance, valve stems / TPMS service, rotation/alignment, disposal

- **TPMS**
